title: Two-state latent wear plus warm-up filter
summary: Recent F1 tire-degradation work models latent pace degradation with state resets at pit stops, while F1 strategy analysis highlights that undercut/overcut outcomes depend not just on worn-tyre decay but also on fresh-tyre warm-up.
rationale: Recent F1 tire-degradation work models latent pace degradation with state resets at pit stops, while F1 strategy analysis highlights that undercut/overcut outcomes depend not just on worn-tyre decay but also on fresh-tyre warm-up. The current handcrafted ratios mostly encode wear, not temporary cold-tyre drag.
implementation_hint: Fit a lightweight forward filter per `Year,Race,Driver` that decomposes lap-time evolution into fuel burn, latent wear state, and a short-lived post-pit warm-up penalty. Export filtered wear level, wear acceleration, warm-up debt, and uncertainty-like residual scale as features. Use only past laps and exclude pit-entry/exit contamination.
expected_effect: Could separate ‘pit now because tyres are gone’ from ‘do not pit yet because the new set will be cold/slow here’, especially on low-deg or hard-warm-up circuits.
risk: If the filter is too flexible or poorly identified, it will just repackage noise. This is also close enough to prior latent-state ideas that it needs a sharper warm-up component to be genuinely new.
